summary.what_happened: Appointment booking latency and errors rose sharply.
summary.customer_impact: Patients experience slow or failed appointment booking.

incident_window.rule: alert.triggered_at (2026-06-01T14:35:52+00:00) minus 30m lookback; deviated series not recovered -> ongoing

severity.explanation: error rate 4.8% and p95 latency 7x baseline on the booking flow

timeline.description: deploy 2026.06.01-1420 on booking-service: Enable payment eligibility enrichment
deploy 2026.06.01-1420 on booking-service: deployment to production: automated deploy
p95_latency_ms first deviated >=2.0x from baseline (450.0 ms -> 3200.0 ms)
WARN: WARN retrying eligibility lookup (attempt 2 of 5)
ERROR: ERROR eligibility lookup timed out after 2000ms
ERROR: eligibility enrichment query timed out
Alert fired: EligibilityTimeout: eligibility lookup timed out after 2000ms
ERROR: EligibilityTimeout: eligibility lookup timed out after 2000ms

evidence.interpretation: booking-service deploy 2026.06.01-1420 landed 11 minutes before latency crossed 2x baseline; timing aligned
eligibility retries escalate to exhaustion starting 14:29
p95 latency first crossed 2x baseline at 14:30
notifications-service stayed at baseline throughout the window
runbook failure mode 'retry amplification after payment changes' matches observed retry warnings

hypotheses.title: Deploy-driven eligibility retries saturating appointments-db
hypotheses.statement: The 14:20 booking-service deploy enabled payment eligibility enrichment whose slow queries and unbounded-looking retries saturated appointments-db, consistent with the latency and error escalation from 14:25.
assumptions: telemetry in the package is complete for the window
recommended_checks: compare booking error rates before and after release 2026.06.01-1420
inspect payment eligibility retry bounds and backoff

missing_data.impact: cannot identify slow spans or failing dependencies

missing_data.impact: flagged by an investigator as limiting its conclusions

safe_mitigation_options.rationale: the runbook documents it as a verified safe fallback

remediation_plans.title: Consider disabling the payment_enrichment feature flag
preconditions: staging fallback verification from 2026-05-28 still applies

steps.action: confirm current payment_enrichment flag state and rollout scope
steps.verification: flag console shows the expected current state
eligibility retry warnings stop within 5 minutes and booking p95 trends toward the 450ms baseline

abort_conditions: booking error rate rises further within 10 minutes of the flag change

remediation_plans.title: Rollback checklist for booking-service release 2026.06.01-1420

steps.action: check whether release 2026.06.01-1420 shipped data migrations or schema changes
steps.verification: release notes and migration directory reviewed

steps.action: roll booking-service back to the previous release
steps.verification: deployed version reports the previous release and appointments-db CPU falls below 60%

abort_conditions: rollback pods crash-loop or error rate exceeds 10%

signals.recovered_when: within 10% of baseline 0.3 % for >= 3 consecutive points
within 10% of baseline 450 ms for >= 3 consecutive points

recovery_verification.re_alert_condition: the original alert 'EligibilityTimeout: eligibility lookup timed out after 2000ms' fires again

checks.detail: SEV-2 is within the numeric ceiling (SEV-2)

communication_drafts.internal_update: SEV-2: appointment booking degraded. No remediation has been executed; mitigation options await human approval.

jira_ticket.summary: Investigate booking latency degradation after the 14:20 deploy
jira_ticket.description: Booking p95 rose 450ms -> 3200ms and errors 0.3% -> 4.8% from 2026-06-01T14:25Z (window start 14:05Z, ongoing). Leading hypothesis (medium confidence): deploy-driven eligibility retry amplification saturating appointments-db. Affected: booking-service, payment-service, appointments-db.

slack_update.text: SEV-2, booking degraded: patients see slow or failed appointment booking. Leading hypothesis (medium confidence): the 14:20 deploy amplified eligibility retries. Checking retry bounds and pre/post deploy error rates next. No remediation has been executed; mitigation options await human approval.

status_page.text: Some users may currently experience slow or failed appointment scheduling. Our team is actively investigating with high priority. Updates will be posted here as we learn more.
